Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated creatinine, proteinuria, microalbuminuria, and abnormal urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity), suggesting significant renal impairment. Elevated chloride and LDL cholesterol are also noted. HbA1c is low, ruling out chronic hyperglycemia. No evidence of urinary infection. Findings are consistent with acute or chronic kidney disease; prompt nephrology evaluation is recommended.
